title: Leji context manifest (leji.json)
description: The machine entrypoint of a Leji context layer. Lives at the repository root as leji.json.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema, for editor validation and tooling.

leji.description: The Leji spec line this context layer targets, e.g. "1.0". The self-naming key follows the OpenAPI convention and identifies the file as a Leji manifest.

name.description: A short, stable identifier for this context layer, e.g. "acme-billing-context".

description.description: One-line summary of what this context layer covers.

rootPath.description: Context root, POSIX path relative to the repository root. Declares where the context layer lives; it does not re-base other paths: all paths in all Leji artifacts resolve from the repository root.

bootProfilePath.description: Path to the boot profile, the agent-agnostic entrypoint every host and person starts from.

categories.description: Logical category to path mapping. Keys are the five category identifiers.

domain.description: Where domain content lives: business language and product semantics.

system.description: Where system content lives: architecture and the invariants every change respects.

practice.description: Where practice content lives: conventions and proven patterns.

governance.description: Where governance content lives: agent guardrails and operating rules.

decisions.description: Where decision records live: dated records of why things are the way they are.

machine.description: Locations of the machine-readable artifacts: index, changelog, profiles, and decision records.

indexPath.description: Path to the generated context index.

changelogPath.description: Path to the machine-readable context changelog.

agents.description: Role to agent-profile binding. Keys are role identifiers (e.g. "thought-partner", "reviewer"); values are paths to agent profile documents. Protocols engage roles; this map decides who fills them.

owners.description: Who is accountable for the context layer's health. Owners answer for the context layer staying current and pruned; they do not author or curate it alone (governance.md).

primary.description: The person accountable for the context layer's currency. Ownerless context layers rot.

continuity.description: Optional. A different person who carries the same accountability when the primary is unavailable or steps away; assisted adoptions should name one before outside help leaves. Naming the primary again provides no continuity, and an agent cannot fill it: owners are accountable people.

conformance.description: The conformance level this context layer claims, checkable by tooling.

claimedLevel.description: The conformance level claimed: core, indexed, governed, or federated.

claimedAt.description: ISO 8601 date the claim was last asserted.

path.description: Where the sibling layer is mounted in this repository, e.g. "context/product/". The sibling's own leji.json lives at this path's root.

name.description: The sibling layer's name; must match the `name` in the sibling's own manifest.

owner.description: The owner of the sibling layer. Ownership stays with the sibling's team; the host never absorbs its content.

role.description: What this sibling layer carries, e.g. "product-side context".

source.description: Upstream repository URL of the sibling layer. Optional; enables stale-pin reporting when git submodule metadata alone isn't available.

mounts.description: Each mount is a docs-only sibling layer at a pinned version, keeping its own repository, ownership, and review gate.

federation.description: Sibling layers mounted into this repository (spec: distribution.md pattern 3). The circle composes ownership; it doesn't centralize it.

vendorAdapters.description: Vendor entrypoint files present in this repository; each must redirect to the boot profile.

docs.description: Presentation preferences read by `leji docs`. Non-normative convenience configuration; presentation itself is out of normative scope.

port.description: Preferred local-preview port for `leji docs --serve`. The --port flag overrides; the default is 5354 (LEJI on a phone keypad).

contact.description: How to reach them, e.g. an email address.

paths.description: One or more repository-root-relative paths where this category's content lives.
